steps.title: Step 1: Set up the tree
steps.body: A bag contains 5 red counters and 3 blue counters (8 total). Two counters are drawn at random **without replacement**. Draw two levels of branches: the first split for the colour of the first counter, the second split (from each first branch) for the colour of the second counter.

steps.title: Step 2: Label the first-draw branches
steps.body:
First draw probabilities, straight from the bag of 8:

$$
P(R_1) = \frac{5}{8}, \qquad P(B_1) = \frac{3}{8}
$$

steps.title: Step 3: Label the second-draw branches (without replacement)
steps.body:
After removing one counter, only 7 remain, and the counts change depending on the first draw:

- After red: $P(R_2 \mid R_1) = \dfrac{4}{7}$, $P(B_2 \mid R_1) = \dfrac{3}{7}$
- After blue: $P(R_2 \mid B_1) = \dfrac{5}{7}$, $P(B_2 \mid B_1) = \dfrac{2}{7}$

steps.title: Step 4: Multiply along branches
Step 5: Add branches for an 'or' event
steps.body:
To find the probability of drawing **one counter of each colour** (in either order), add the two paths that match, since different paths are mutually exclusive:

$$
P(\text{one of each}) = P(R_1 \cap B_2) + P(B_1 \cap R_2) = \frac{15}{56} + \frac{15}{56} = \frac{30}{56} = \frac{15}{28} \approx 0.536
$$

As a check, all four end probabilities should sum to 1: $\frac{20+15+15+6}{56} = \frac{56}{56} = 1$. ✓
